title: OSI, TCP/IP, Encapsulation, and Traffic Evidence · Operations Brief: Net Layer Addressing
summary: Trace a client request through host, switch, router, firewall, service, and return path using captures and diagrams. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net layer addressing increment in a layered packet-flow diagnosis. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish MAC, IP, port, name, and application identifiers by scope, lifetime, and forwarding purpose.
Trace data, segments or datagrams, packets, frames, and bits through encapsulation, forwarding, and decapsulation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
